title: Personal app user profile / name setup wizard + later editing in Settings
parent_epic: FD-264 Personal + Pro App design-partner validation
directives: Patrick 2026-06-11: use /ui-planner for the user details component (wizard + Settings UI design)

oracle.note: On disk + ratified; commits with the first build commit (no referee/gate-ancestry requirement in this project)

oracle_proposal.ratification_note: Patrick ratified C1-C11 + C13 explicitly. C12 (human walk) retained on the human track - not skipped (no explicit skip gesture); Patrick may drop it.

criteria.text: Fresh Personal app account with no profile set: a profile setup flow appears at first launch / first diagram use capturing name and birth date; it does not reappear after completion
Skipping the wizard leaves the app fully usable, the wizard does not nag on every launch, and extraction still runs to a valid result with no name set (no crash/500)
Empty name or invalid birth date cannot be saved from wizard or Settings: visible validation, no silent garbage stored
Wizard-entered name lands on the user's own (primary) person node; birth date is stored as a BIRTH EVENT on that node (not a scalar field); both survive full app kill+relaunch with server reload
Settings shows the current profile values; editing them persists server-side (diagram version advances; reload shows the new values)
A diagram created before this feature opens cleanly; profile can be set from Settings and lands on the primary node (or a deterministic fallback when none is marked); no crash
The new profile fields survive both app save paths: a save/merge from the other app does not silently drop them (the known two-location field-sync hazard)

With profile set, the real extraction request context carries the speaker identity - the user's REAL NAME as the speaker label (not the generic 'Client') plus birth date - observable in the captured prompt on the sandbox server

Duplicate-proband class is dead: 3 consecutive real-LLM extraction/rebuild runs on a named-proband diagram emit zero duplicate people for the speaker (no fabricated unnamed/'Client' person alongside the user's own node)
Name set AFTER discussions were already extracted: a subsequent re-extraction/rebuild does not fabricate a proband duplicate
Per-discussion extraction quality stays within the established +/-0.05 F1 band vs baseline after the context/prompt change
The wizard and Settings profile flow looks and feels right (per the ui-planner design spec) and correctly describes the user on a real diagram - human walk
Chat UI shows the user's real name as the speaker label instead of the generic 'Client'

resolved_decisions: Birth date storage: BIRTH EVENT on the primary node (Patrick 2026-06-15: 'it must be a birth event. easy to do'). Diagram already models birth as a dated event.
Transcript label: real name everywhere - extraction prompts AND chat UI (Patrick 2026-06-15: 'yes, real name too'). Added C13.

Referee: this project never built a deterministic checker; FD-321 is a spin-off blocking FD-338 (itself not closed). Coordinator decision: drive every machine-checkable criterion to a real measured result and record the evidence in this state file (same as FD-338), no referee build. Standing project convention.

criteria.text: Fresh account, no profile: setup flow appears at first launch / first diagram, captures name + birth date, never reappears after completion
criteria.evidence: Real Personal app (worktree code, offscreen, isolated ephemeral server, logged-in seeded user): userDetailsWizard element present + visible=true on launch; after completing the wizard the element is unloaded (find_element success=false) so it won't reappear. Driven via mcpbridge 2026-06-15 (/tmp/fd321_validate.py).

criteria.text: Skipping the wizard leaves the app usable, no nagging on relaunch; extraction still runs to a valid result with no name set (no crash/500)
criteria.evidence: Real app: clicked the real Skip button -> wizard unloads (find_element false), appState stays 'running', and no person was created (empty scene). 'extraction still runs with no name' is covered by btcopilot WP2 graceful-degradation unit test. mcpbridge 2026-06-15.

criteria.text: Empty name / invalid birth date cannot be saved (wizard or Settings): visible validation, no silent garbage
criteria.evidence: Real app: userDetailsForm.valid=false with empty first name; after setting first name via the real field, valid=true (Get Started gates on it). Date control (PK.DatePickerButtons) only accepts real dates so invalid dates can't be entered. mcpbridge 2026-06-15.

criteria.text: Wizard name lands on the primary person node; birth date stored as a BIRTH EVENT on that node; both survive full app kill+relaunch with server reload
criteria.evidence: Real app: typed a date into the real EventForm-style date field -> form birthYear derived =1985; clicked the real Get Started button -> a person named 'Jordan' appears in the live scene. PERSISTENCE PROVEN: relaunched a second instance against the SAME server -> 'Jordan' still in the scene, and the wizard did NOT reappear (named node -> shouldPromptProfile false). Birth-event creation on the node unit-tested. mcpbridge 2026-06-15.

criteria.text: Settings shows current profile values; edits persist server-side (diagram version advances; reload shows new values)
criteria.evidence: Real app: opened drawer -> account entry -> profile popup; profileForm pre-populated with the saved 'Jordan'/'Lee'; edited first name to 'Jordana', clicked the real Save -> the live scene person renamed to 'Jordana'. Server-reload-shows-new-values not separately re-driven but save uses the same persisted path proven in C4. mcpbridge 2026-06-15.

criteria.text: Pre-feature diagram opens cleanly; profile settable from Settings; lands on primary node or deterministic fallback; no crash
criteria.evidence: Observable behaviors live-verified on the real app (mcpbridge, isolated server): a diagram with no named/marked primary opens with appState='running' (no crash); Settings shows blank and does not crash; saving a name lands it on the node. 'Settable lands on the EXISTING primary node' is live-proven by C5 (renamed the existing primary Jordan->Jordana, no duplicate). The deterministic fallback SELECTION (lowest-id person when none marked primary) is pure selection logic, unit-verified (test_primaryPerson_falls_back_when_none_marked, test_primaryPerson_prefers_marked_over_id) - the correct venue; a harness would only re-run the same deterministic function.

criteria.text: Profile data survives BOTH app save paths (two-location field-sync hazard): a save/merge from the other app does not drop it
criteria.evidence: Verified-by-construction via diff audit on the real repos: FD-321 added NO new DiagramData/Person field (name uses existing Person.name/last_name; birth is an existing Birth Event), and Scene.diagramData() + serverfilemanagermodel.applyChange() are unchanged (git diff shows zero additions there). The field-sync hazard only exists for NEW fields, so profile data provably cannot be dropped by either save path.

criteria.text: DESCOPED 2026-06-15 (Patrick): feeding identity into the extraction PROMPT is prompt development that belongs to FD-338's extraction/dedup work, not this rules-based intake wizard. The prompt-identity block was removed. FD-321 only provides the named node + the named speaker label.
DESCOPED 2026-06-15 (Patrick): duplicate-proband elimination is FD-338's extraction/dedup concern. FD-321 only names the node + speaker; whether the extractor dedups well is FD-338. e2e test removed.
DESCOPED 2026-06-15 (Patrick): re-extraction dedup behavior is FD-338, not this wizard.
DESCOPED 2026-06-15 (Patrick): no extraction-prompt change in FD-321 anymore, so there is no F1 regression to guard - the reason this criterion existed is gone.
Wizard + Settings flow looks/feels right (per ui-planner design spec) and correctly describes the user on a real diagram

The user's real name (not 'Client') appears on every surface that references them: chat transcript speaker label (Speaker.name from the node) + the account profile (now linked, see C14). App chat bubbles carry no name label; no other hardcoded 'Client' placeholder in the app.
criteria.evidence: Transcript label from the node (discussions.py subject_display_name). Account name linked (C14). Codebase sweep found no other client-name placeholder surface in the app; fdserver prompts reference 'the speaker' generically (prompt-side, out of scope).

criteria.text: Account name <-> own-diagram node name are linked (two-way), scoped to the user's OWN (free) diagram so a professional's client files never sync to the clinician's account (Patrick 2026-06-16)
criteria.evidence: Real app + isolated server: wizard PRE-FILLS firstName from the account ('Test'); saving 'Zelda Quill' WROTE BACK to the server user record (read from sandbox SQLite: first_name/last_name updated). Scope guard unit-tested: a client file (non-free diagram) neither pre-fills from nor writes to the account; nothing-loaded = the user's own default scene. familydiagram personal suite 176 green. e2e /tmp/fd321_link_e2e.py.

agent_track.note: No referee in project; closed evidence-based. All agent criteria agent-verified or deferred (C8-C11 -> FD-338). Exhausted guard green.

human_track.note: C12 only. Script authored by sub-agent; running structure/handoff guards before hand-off.

findings.summary: First build pass used a bare PK.DatePicker for birth date - unfocusable, could not be clicked into; clunky UX. Fixed: reuses the EventForm PK.DatePickerButtons+PK.DatePicker pair (editable/focusable). Structure verified (parses/mounts, 14 form tests pass); interactive focus/click confirmation pending the real-app walk.
findings.repro: Open the wizard/profile form; try to click into or focus the birth date field

findings.summary: First build pass had no tab-focus order. Fixed: KeyNavigation chain First->Last->birth date field, initial focus on First name. Structure verified; live tab/click confirmation pending the real-app walk.
findings.repro: Tab through the form fields; focus does not advance in order

findings.summary: Birth-date tumbler was always visible (shouldShow:true). FIXED: removed shouldShow so the tumbler collapses until the date field is focused, matching EventForm. Verified the date field still accepts typed input on the real app (C4-birth).
findings.repro: Open the profile form; date tumbler occupied the card permanently

findings.summary: Both UserDetailsForm instances (wizard + Settings) embed the name fields with the SAME objectNames (firstNameField/lastNameField). Not fatal today (the wizard unloads before Settings opens, so they are never co-mounted), but a latent test-bridge ambiguity of the same class as FD-338 F-005. Consider namespacing the field objectNames per host.
findings.repro: Mount wizard and Settings profile simultaneously; find_element firstNameField is ambiguous

findings.summary: AccountDrawer ACCOUNT entry had no objectName, so the test bridge could not reach Settings. FIXED: added objectName 'accountEntry' (also enables the C5 harness path).
findings.repro: Try to click the account entry via the bridge before the fix

findings.summary: Human walk (Patrick): the Personal app does not render the family-diagram canvas, so the named node can only be SEEN in the Pro app opened against the same backend; the Pro app loads the server version on open and does not live-sync a concurrent Personal-app edit (re-open required). Environment/script reality, NOT a product bug.
findings.repro: Set name in Personal wizard; open the free diagram in a Pro app on the same server -> node visible after (re-)open.

deferred_risk.what: Extraction-prompt identity + duplicate-proband dedup + F1 band (C8,C9,C10,C11) removed from FD-321
deferred_risk.why: Patrick: this is a rules-based intake wizard, not prompt development; duplicate-proband dedup is FD-338's extraction concern. The prompt block + its regression tests were removed.

history.note: New unit (adopt guard: no branch/PR/prior state). Worktrees: btcopilot, familydiagram (fdserver deferred until build shows prompt-override need). 3-critic adversarial pass; proposal posted to FD-321 (comment 10389); Patrick ratified C1-C11+C13, amended birth-date->birth-event and real-name-everywhere (added C13); C12 retained on human track. Oracle written + ratified. Ticket -> In Progress.

Build opened. ui-planner ran: single-screen wizard chosen (variant A) over paged, side-by-side prototypes. Spec familydiagram/doc/ui-specs/user-details.md; FINAL demo + decision-log entry written. Work packages defined; implementation dispatched.

WP1+WP2 implemented by parallel workers; coordinator independently re-ran suites against worktree code: familydiagram personal 173, btcopilot personal 207 + schema 101, all green, no regressions. UNIT-LEVEL PRE-SCREEN ONLY. fdserver not needed. Q-C3 date-validation fork surfaced to Patrick.

Patrick UX feedback: reuse EventForm date control + tab order + collapse-until-focused tumbler. Form revised (PK.DatePickerButtons+PK.DatePicker, KeyNavigation chain, shouldShow removed). Re-verified: 14 form tests + 173 personal pass.

AGENT TRACK opened. Reconstructed the missing worktree test-harness env (workspace pyproject + uv sync venv; copied gitignored pepper.py/build_uuid.py + compiled _pkdiagram.so). Drove the REAL Personal app offscreen via mcpbridge against an isolated ephemeral server (never prod 8888). PROVEN on the live app: C1 (wizard appears+unloads), C3 (empty-name gate real), C4 (typed date->birthYear 1985; real Get Started click lands person 'Jordan' on the live scene).

More live proofs: C2 (Skip), C4-persist (relaunch shared server), C5 (Settings shows/edits/persists). Patrick: stripped out-of-scope extraction prompt development (descope C8-C11 to FD-338); kept rules-based label rename. C7 N/A (no new field added).

Patrick: link account name <-> own-diagram node, scoped to the free diagram (client files excluded). Implemented two-way (pre-fill + write-back), verified on the live app (pre-fill 'Test'; save wrote 'Zelda Quill' to the server user record). Adversarial review found 2 major bugs (clear-name desync, logout-on-repull) + minors; all fixed + tested. Final: btcopilot 349, familydiagram 178 green. Both PRs updated. Only C12 (human walk) remains.

Human walk in progress (live sandbox: Personal app + Pro app on one ephemeral server). A1-A4 pass. DIRECT server-DB evidence: free_diagram id=1 holds person id=3 'Patrick' primary=True + birth event 1980-05-11; account = 'Patrick Stinson' (wizard write-back). Pro app shows the node after (re-)open. Script revised to the two-app reality (node=Pro, wizard/Settings/chat-label/drawer=Personal) via the script sub-agent.

work_packages.what: UserDetailsForm.qml (reusable) + first-launch wizard (UserDetailsWizard.qml) mount in PersonalContainer + ProfileSettingsPage.qml + controller persistence (name on primary node, birth date as a birth event, personalProfilePrompted pref) + 14 app tests
work_packages.status: implemented; unit-green (PRE-SCREEN only, not real-app e2e). Coordinator re-ran: familydiagram personal 173 passed against worktree code, no regressions.

work_packages.what: _speaker_identity_for_prompt() injects real name + birth date into pass-1 extraction context with do-not-duplicate-speaker instruction; Subject speaker label derived from primary person name (fallback 'Client' when unset) at discussion create + every chat turn; dedup safety net kept; 9 mocked tests
work_packages.status: implemented; unit-green (PRE-SCREEN only, not real-LLM). Coordinator re-ran: btcopilot personal 207 + schema 101 passed against worktree code, no regressions. fdserver: NOT needed (identity block appended to assembled prompt string, reaches LLM regardless of override).

build_open_questions.status: RESOLVED 2026-06-15: Patrick directed reusing the real PK.DatePickerButtons+PK.DatePicker pair (EventForm idiom). That control cannot produce an invalid date, so C3 is satisfied-by-construction; date validation reduces to first-name-required. No free-text invalid-date path.

validation_env_notes: FD-321 worktrees were missing the test-harness env (not created by git worktree). Reconstructed for real-app validation: /Users/patrick/worktrees/FD-321/pyproject.toml (uv workspace, members btcopilot+familydiagram) + uv sync (.venv); copied gitignored build artifacts into the worktree: familydiagram/pkdiagram/pepper.py, pkdiagram/build_uuid.py, and the compiled _pkdiagram.cpython-311-darwin.so into the worktree .venv site-packages (same C++ ext, unchanged by FD-321). App driven offscreen via mcpserver TestInstance with ephemeral_server=True (NEVER production 8888). Drivers: /tmp/fd321_uidrive.py, /tmp/fd321_validate.py.

agent_track_ui_PROVEN_live: C4 save name+birth + persists across relaunch + no-nag
agent_track_ui_partial: C6 (no-crash+settable shown live; fallback-to-existing-person unit-tested only)
remaining_agent_ui: C7 both-app save paths (needs Pro app)
C13 chat label real name (unit-tested; live render needs a discussion)
remaining_backend: C8 prompt carries identity (unit-tested; live capture pending)
C9-C11 real-LLM extraction (dup-proband dead, F1 band) - expensive, not run

parked_on: Patrick's C12 walkthrough. Human script at doc/workstreams/fd-321-human-script.md (passing guards before hand-off). Agent track green; C8-C11 deferred to FD-338.
